audit_items.proof_trace: In quicklistRotate, when the quicklist has exactly one node (len==1), ziplistGet sets value = p+entry.headersize — a pointer into node->zl. quicklistPushHead then calls __ziplistInsert, which invokes ziplistResize (zrealloc) on the same buffer at L790 of ziplist.c, potentially freeing and relocating it; the subsequent memcpy(p, s, slen) at L833 reads from s==value, which is now a dangling pointer. The code explicitly re-fetches p for the single-node case (L1307-1309) but fails to re-fetch or copy value before the realloc, leaving a use-after-free read on any non-integer-encoded string element. Reachability is uncertain: quicklistRotate has no callers in the server command handlers (t_list.c, server.c) in this codebase version, only appearing in internal test code.
audit_items.attack_scenario: If quicklistRotate is exposed via a command or module, an attacker who pushes non-integer string elements into a Redis list small enough to fit in a single quicklist node and triggers rotation (e.g., via RPOPLPUSH to self or a future LROTATE command) can cause the server to read from freed ziplist memory, potentially corrupting the head element written into the list.
